blocks.heading: The two pure-authoring products ‚Äî render a hand-written spec or doc straight into self-contained HTML, with no codebase scan.

rows.value: 5 ‚Äî two thin CLIs over `src/` (one extra render path, one extra block model, one lint floor)
Unlike `recap` / `atlas`, the input is **hand-authored blocks JSON**, not a scan. `spec` renders an approval-focused design spec; `doc` is the general illustrated-doc renderer.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "Two thin CLIs over the shared engine."
bigIdea.sub: `doc` reuses **core's generic `Block` model** directly ‚Äî `bin/doc.ts` just promotes mermaid fences and calls `assemble`, with almost no domain code of its own. `spec` adds its **own approval-focused block vocabulary** (`SpecBlock` ‚Äî tldr, decisions, scope, rollout, approve) plus a dedicated `assembleSpec` renderer and a completeness `lintSpec` floor. Both ride the same diagram pipeline.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: `doc` is the thin one ‚Äî it leans on core + diagrams with no domain-specific model. `spec` is the substantial one: its own block model, its own renderer, and its own lint. Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: spec CLI
cards.purpose: `bin/spec.ts` ‚Äî parses `--blocks <spec.json> --out <dir>`, maps page-level fields to `SpecOpts`, calls `assembleSpec`, and writes `spec.html` + re-writes `spec.json` so the folder re-renders in place.

cards.name: doc CLI
cards.purpose: `bin/doc.ts` ‚Äî the thinner CLI: reads `--blocks` as core's generic `Block[]`, promotes mermaid fences, calls core's `assemble`, writes `doc.html` + `blocks.json`. No spec model, no lint.

cards.name: spec block model
cards.purpose: `src/spec-blocks.ts` ‚Äî the `SpecBlock` union: the approval vocabulary (`tldr`, `decisions`, `scope`, `rollout`, `approve`, `risks`, `done`, `reference`, ‚Ä¶) plus id-uniqueness, chapter labelling, and diagram collection.

cards.name: spec render
cards.purpose: `src/assemble-spec.ts` ‚Äî `assembleSpec` renders each block to a `<section>`, derives the sidebar outline + progress rail from the chapters, and reuses the recap's `renderDiagramCard` for diagrams.

cards.name: spec lint
cards.purpose: `src/lint-spec.ts` ‚Äî the demo-standard completeness floor: warns (never crashes) when the lead, decisions, or scope are missing, scaling stricter for large specs.

blocks.note: `doc` has no card of its own in `src/` ‚Äî it borrows core's `assemble` and the diagrams domain's `promoteMermaidFences` wholesale. The four spec pieces (`bin/spec.ts` + the three `src/` files) are what this domain actually owns.

blocks.title: Two entrypoints, one core
blocks.intro: `spec.json` flows through `bin/spec.ts` ‚Üí `assembleSpec` (using `spec-blocks` + `lintSpec`); `blocks.json` flows through `bin/doc.ts` ‚Üí core's `assemble`. Both share the diagrams pipeline (`promote-mermaid` for doc, `renderAll` for both) and the core renderers.
blocks.callout: The lone amber box ‚Äî `assembleSpec` ‚Äî is the only substantial renderer authoring owns. Everything below it (`core / assemble`, the diagram pipeline) is borrowed; `bin/doc.ts` is just wiring.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece ‚Äî what it does, its files and exports, and how it connects to core, diagrams, and recap.

components.name: spec CLI
detail: `bin/spec.ts` is the visual-spec entrypoint. It `parseArgs` for `--blocks <spec.json>` and `--out <dir>` (both required, both must be absolute), reads the JSON as a `SpecDoc` (page options + a `blocks` array), maps the page-level fields onto `SpecOpts`, and calls [`assembleSpec`](#c-spec-render). It writes `<out>/spec.html` and re-writes `<out>/spec.json`, so the doc folder stays self-contained and re-renders in place ‚Äî mirroring `recap --blocks`. Author warnings collected via `onWarn` are printed after the write.

files.name: bin/spec.ts
files.desc: Arg parsing, `SpecDoc` ‚Üí `SpecOpts` mapping, and the write-back of `spec.html` + `spec.json`.

exports.name: main()
exports.desc: The CLI body ‚Äî reads, assembles, writes; exits 2 on usage errors.

components.name: doc CLI
detail: `bin/doc.ts` is the thinnest product in the toolkit. It reads `--blocks` as core's **generic `Block[]`** (no spec model involved), runs [`promoteMermaidFences`](../domain-diagrams/domain-diagrams.html) to lift ```mermaid``` code fences into real diagram blocks, then hands the result to core's [`assemble`](../domain-core/domain-core.html#c-assemble). It writes `doc.html` plus a copy of `blocks.json` into the `--out` directory so the doc is self-contained and re-renders in place. There is no `assemble-doc`, no `doc-blocks`, and no lint ‚Äî doc is wiring over core + diagrams.

files.name: bin/doc.ts
files.desc: Reads `Block[]`, promotes mermaid fences, calls core `assemble`, writes `doc.html` + `blocks.json`.

exports.name: main()
exports.desc: The CLI body ‚Äî promote + assemble + write; throws on missing `--blocks`.

connections.body: core's generic `Block` model directly ‚Äî no domain-specific block types

components.name: spec block model
detail: The `SpecBlock` discriminated union ‚Äî the approval-focused vocabulary that distinguishes a spec from a generic doc: `tldr` (the lead), `diagram` (info-flow hero / where-it-fits), `components` (with the optional two-layer anatomy explainer), `fits`, `decisions` (the highest-value content for an approver), `scope` (in/out boundaries), `rollout` (gated phases), `done`, `risks`, `approve` (the commit / scrutinize / open-questions band), `reference` (drill-down drawers), and the `spec-prose` escape hatch. Helpers derive the page structure: `isChapter` (tldr + reference are not numbered chapters), `assertUniqueSpecIds`, `chapterLabel`, and `collectSpecDiagrams` / `toDiagramBlock` to feed the shared diagram pipeline.

files.name: spec-blocks.ts
files.desc: The `SpecBlock` union, every block interface, and the chapter / id / diagram helpers.

exports.name: SpecBlock
exports.desc: The block union ‚Äî `TldrBlock | DecisionsBlock | ScopeBlock | RolloutBlock | ApproveBlock | ‚Ä¶`.

exports.name: collectSpecDiagrams() ¬∑ toDiagramBlock()
exports.desc: Maps spec `diagram` blocks into core's `DiagramBlock` for the shared pipeline.

exports.name: isChapter() ¬∑ chapterLabel() ¬∑ assertUniqueSpecIds()
exports.desc: Drive the numbered outline, the progress rail, and id-collision detection.

connections.body: core's generic `Block` model that [doc CLI](#c-doc-cli) uses

components.name: spec render
detail: `assembleSpec(blocks, opts)` is the spec page builder. It asserts unique ids, loads `review.css` + `spec.css` + the shared viewer JS, collects the spec diagrams and renders them via the diagrams domain's `renderAll`, then runs the page through `validateSpecOpts` and [`lintSpec`](#c-spec-lint) (warnings only). Each block becomes a `<section>` via a per-type renderer; the sidebar outline and the progress rail are derived from the chapters (the rail is injected right after the TL;DR). For diagram blocks it reuses the **recap's** `renderDiagramCard` ‚Äî the one piece of review-shell rendering authoring borrows.

files.name: assemble-spec.ts
files.desc: Topbar / sidebar / rail, the per-block `<section>` renderers, and the page shell.

exports.name: assembleSpec()
exports.desc: ‚Üí a full self-contained HTML string for the spec page.

exports.name: SpecOpts
exports.desc: Page options: title, phase, status, date, complexity, related, meta, outDir, excalidraw.

components.name: spec lint
detail: `lintSpec(blocks)` is the demo-standard completeness floor ‚Äî a mechanical backstop that keeps every spec at the bar. It returns warning strings (never throws): no `tldr` lead, an empty TL;DR, missing `decisions` (the load-bearing content for an approver) or a decision lacking a `why`, and a missing `scope` block. For a *large* spec (‚â• 5 chapters) it also expects a hero `diagram`, a `rollout`, and an `approve` band, and nudges for a named rejected-alternative on contested decisions. `assembleSpec` surfaces these through `onWarn`.

files.name: lint-spec.ts
files.desc: The heuristic checks (lead / decisions / scope, scaled stricter past `LARGE_CHAPTERS = 5`).

exports.name: lintSpec()
exports.desc: ‚Üí `string[]` of completeness warnings, in document priority order.

connections.body: heuristics, not hard errors ‚Äî the page still renders, the author is nudged

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: Authoring owns the **spec block model** ‚Äî the approval-focused vocabulary in `spec-blocks.ts`. `doc` owns no model of its own; it borrows core's generic `Block`.

rows.name: TldrBlock
rows.desc: `type: "tldr"` ‚Äî the lead: a one-line heading, What / Why / Closes / Size rows, and an optional big-idea line.

rows.name: SpecDiagramBlock
rows.desc: `type: "diagram"` ‚Äî a standalone d2/mermaid section (info-flow hero, where-it-fits); its id is both section and diagram id.

rows.name: ComponentsBlock ¬∑ ComponentCard
rows.desc: `type: "components"` ‚Äî the component grid, with the optional two-layer anatomy (factual / perceived) explainer.

rows.name: FitsBlock
rows.desc: `type: "fits"` ‚Äî the where-it-fits chain plus an optional new-vs-reused layer stack.

rows.name: DecisionsBlock ¬∑ DecisionItem
rows.desc: `type: "decisions"` ‚Äî Q / A / why / rejected cards; the highest-value content for an approver.

rows.name: ScopeBlock
rows.desc: `type: "scope"` ‚Äî in-scope goals vs out-of-scope anti-goals, with optional defer targets.

rows.name: RolloutBlock ¬∑ PhaseItem
rows.desc: `type: "rollout"` ‚Äî gated sub-phases, each with a scope and an acceptance gate.

rows.name: ApproveBlock
rows.desc: `type: "approve"` ‚Äî the sign-off band: what you're approving, what to scrutinize, and open non-blocking questions.

rows.name: ReferenceBlock ¬∑ SpecProseBlock
rows.desc: `type: "reference"` (collapsed drill-down drawers) and `type: "spec-prose"` (the unmodeled-content escape hatch).

blocks.note: All of these compose into `SpecBlock`. Borrows (never owns): `DiagramBlock` / `DiagramKind` / `TabsBlock` from `core`; the generic `Block` model that `doc` renders.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: Two CLIs out; the shared engine, the diagram pipeline, and one recap shell piece in.

exposes.note: the spec page builder, used by the CLI

blocks.note: `doc` leans entirely on `core` (`assemble`, `Block`) and `diagrams` (`promoteMermaidFences`). `spec` adds its own model + renderer + lint, and reuses exactly one review-shell piece: the recap's `renderDiagramCard` for its diagram sections.
